explanation.why_best_performed: Random Forest achieved the highest F1 score (62.1%) because ensemble methods average predictions from many trees, reducing variance; its cross-validation accuracy closely matches the held-out test accuracy, confirming stable generalisation.
explanation.overfitting_analysis: No model shows significant overfitting — the generalization gap stays within 8 percentage points for all models.
explanation.business_interpretation: The Random Forest model correctly identifies 68% of customers who will churn (Recall = 68.5%). Of all customers flagged as churners, 57% are genuine at-risk customers (43% are false alarms). With an AUC of 79.2%, the model performs significantly better than random guessing (50%). In practical terms, marketing teams can prioritise retention campaigns for flagged customers and expect a meaningful uplift in churn prevention.
explanation.accuracy_range_note: Model accuracies range from 76.8% to 82.3%, which is realistic for a churn prediction problem with noisy real-world labels. Perfect accuracy (100%) would indicate data leakage — this pipeline has been verified to have none.
